name: Lonnie Liston Smith
abstract: Lonnie Liston Smith Jr. (born December 28, 1940) is an American jazz, soul, and funk pianist and keyboardist who played with such jazz artists as Pharoah Sanders and Miles Davis before forming Lonnie Liston Smith and the Cosmic Echoes, recording a number of albums widely regarded as classics in the fusion, smooth jazz and acid jazz genre…

interviews.title: Lonnie Liston Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 9, 2021

concerts.label: Lonnie Liston Smith and the Cosmic Echoes
Hubert Laws, Lonnie Liston Smith, and Earl Klugh

about_quotes.speaker: Cascadu
about_quotes.evidence: Him, Lonnie Liston Smith, you know, and I just met Lonnie. I drove — I hadn’t seen or heard from Lonnie in over thirty years... to see and meet one of the guys who I revered for his cosmic — he was my cosmic jazz master.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cascadu interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Lonnie Liston Smith. By April he was gone. Next thing I know, “I’m playing it myself.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Oh yeah, Lonnie Liston Smith. Lonnie Liston Smith plays organ, doesn't he? ... I’ve got a recording that you recorded there. ... Eddie Henderson, yourself, Gary Bartz, Lonnie Liston Smith, Dom Um Romao on percussion
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
